Abacus.lore: Abacus was a gnome of legend of the Gate Era. He, along with the Mist Titan Kha-Ymor, was credited with slaying the dragon Esuth, as told in the book Folk Tales found in Visken's Lair.

Agent Viira.lore: Viira is an orc from Bloodcity Bay in the employ of Askanii-Reeds Trading Company, a Freestrider organization. She is found outside of Visken's Lair, south of Alfoz.

Akzel Mâddson.lore: Akzel Mâddson is a Dwarf archeologist.

Alfoz.lore: Alfoz B.E. is a dwarf and a leading figure in Tolstad's Azgalist movement.

Ambassador Ivcx Liuvben.lore: Ambassador Ivcx is a Celestial of the Golden Horde. He can temporarily be found in the Askanii-Reeds Trading Company Building as part of the Freestrider quest Capital Dominion.

Arn.lore: Arn is a human child working at the Pillar Kiosk next to the Pillar of Jor in Tolstad. He sells equipable newspapers and magazines.

Big Leader Gourgalash.lore: Big Leader Gourgalash is a Frost Troll that can be found in the Undercoast Path. He is first encountered while asleep, and will not wake up unless the Cleric approaches or attacks him.

Borgo Darrow.lore: Borgo Darrow was the first of the noblemen House Darrow and the first paladin of the god Urth. He founded the Urthguard, and was said to be brave and invulnerable in battle.

Braxo.lore: Braxo is an imp legal scribe in Tolstad, and a minor but helpful character in the demo. He sits on a pillar south-west of Visken's Lair.

Brrr.lore: Brrr is a sentient mushroom encountered in the Undercoast Path. He is first encountered while frozen in an Esoteric Pocket created by a frost troll.

Caelum Diminorii.lore: Caelum Diminorii was a member of the Diminorii and a legendary figure. He was known as the First Arcanist.

Doug Doulin Dim.lore: Doug Doulin Dim, the Drake Slayer King, was the first human emperor on the Coast. He got his title from his tendency to slay dragons and liberate their human slaves.

Esuth.lore: Esuth was an ancient dragon of historical importance. He was discussed most prominently in the book Folk Tales found in Visken's Lair.

Ettir.lore: Ettir is a city-sworn angel of Tolstad, in service of Magister Darrow. She can be found in front of his house, highest part of Tolstad North.

Ever-Glad.lore: Ever-Glad is a Trifler. He can be found in the Old Prison far below Tolstad.

Fillius Prax.lore: Fillius Prax was a leading figure of Arcanist ideology, a mage and an author of Autobiography Prax. His descendant Rubrick Prax is the head of The Arcane Circle.

Fourth Apprentice of Jor.lore: The Fourth Apprentice of Jor, or the Nameless Apprentice, was one of Jor's original apprentices.

Frank, Son of Frank.lore: A name that's mentioned as a witness for the case of The Tea Shop that Blew Up, injured during the explosion. Learning the name starts the quest The Star Witness.
related: The Tea Shop that Blew Up

Freydis.lore: Freydis is a character with a short role in the demo. She can be found in The Holes.

Geesus.lore: Geesus was a titan who lived in Askan during the Fade Era. He resembled a giant goose.

Gods.lore: Walking gods (or demi-gods) of The Coast are ascended mortals who carried divinity of the original gods residing outside of The Coast.

Gorgorian.lore: Gorgorian was a Archdevil of the Gate Era.

Gorm Skaldeman.lore: Gorm Skaldeman is the last appointed paladin of Urth. He became the face of the Party Of Norvik, but went missing months prior to the events of the game.

Haelon Bondavol.lore: Haelon Bondavol is a demi-lich residing in the Doom below Tolstad. Through a successful DC 24 Intelligence check, the spell Speak with Dead can be learned from him.

Hierophant Ohpidana.lore: Hierophant Ohpidana is a powerful archdruid in the Undercoast. Ohpidana's chief concern is of "keeping balance" in the Undersea by stopping any powers from developing a hold of the region, both dominant powers of The Coast and civilizations in the Undercoast.

Kha-Ymor.lore: Kha-Ymor was a Mist Titan with historical importance during the Gate Era. He was discussed most prominently in the book Folk Tales found in Visken's Lair.

Kraaid.lore: Kraaid is a crocoliskfolk who lives in The Sewers. He is a cook and hunter, and sells Slogdogs, sausages made of a meat with unknown origin.

Lord Patriarch Âkesam.lore: Lord Âkesam is the current Lord Patriarch of Norvik, the commander of city forces and highest-ranking cleric of Urth. He is the leader of the Party Of Norvik.

Miska Sageleaf.lore: Miska Sageleaf (often referred to as Lady Sageleaf) is the leader of the Halfway Bridge Tribe and administrator of Lower Tolstad.
related: The Tea Shop that Blew Up

Miss Nearly.lore: Miss Nearly is a goblin bard.

Modissa.lore: Modissa is a human and a member of the Azgalian Worker's Party in Tolstad.

Moongore.lore: Moongore is a bugbear enforcer of Miska Sageleaf. Nessan instructs The Cleric to find her to get the Goodapple spell.

Nessan.lore: Nes'Wikkala (also known as Nessan) is a Pimeä Elves druid that came to Tolstad from the Undercoast. She resides in the Goblin Garden

Nicholas Darrow.lore: Nicholas Darrow is the Coinlord of Norvik and the magistrate of Tolstad. As a Coinlord, he handles minting, trade, financing of public works, and tithes.

Olzis.lore: Olzis is a Gynosphinx who lives in the Drunk Sphinx tavern. She is the tavern's namesake.

Oyugüi.lore: Oyugüi is a Kargameshian Halfling Vendor found outside the Temple of Urth.

Pinja.lore: Pínja'Athena is an enigmatic elf found behind a house in Tolstad North, silently looking at River Torna. Pinja speaks in cryptic, wistful phrases, and as such it is hard to characterize her.

Razz.lore: Razz is a goblin mercenary. He is a goblin supremacist, and will not talk to you unless you have Snell as a companion.

Rixbiter.lore: Rixbiter is the Tiefling proprietor of the Drunk Sphinx, a tavern in Tolstad.

Rollo Malach.lore: Stenbusken is the leader of the Norvikian Resistance. He can usually be found in the Rollermill or Tavern with his friends.

Rubrick Prax.lore: Rubrick Prax of House Prax is according to Intellgence the last living member of the magocratic family. He is the leader of The Arcane Circle, an Arcanist political group.

Seagull.lore: A giant seagull can be found in the northernmost bridge of Tolstad.
related: The Tea Shop that Blew Up

Simii.lore: Simii is a dwarf from Tolstad, part of the Azgalists and seen with Alfoz during Circle Square Conflict. His title says "son of Strokeback".

Sir Triban.lore: Sir Triban is a black cat located in South Tolstad near Our Lady's Apartments.

Snell.lore: Snell is Cleric's goblin companion investigating the tea shop explosion on behalf of the HWBT. He is a possible party member, and the one most likely to be encountered first.

Snurre Appledent.lore: Snurre Appledent is a halfling scholar in Waterlane. He is a Scholar of the Fordnip University, specializing in the field of AIS(Alignment Intent Studies).

Stenbusken.lore: Stenbusken is a member of the Norvikian Resistance. He can usually be found in the Rollermill or Tavern with his friends.

The Cleric.lore: The Cleric is Esoteric Ebb's player character. His full name is Ragn Hemlin, though most of the narration in the game refers to him as The Cleric.

The Duck.lore: The Duck is a solemn armored warrior found in the Temple of Urth.

Thomas Darrow.lore: Thomas Darrow was a nobleman of House Darrow and the uncle of Nicholas Darrow. He was a vampire.

Tinnitul.lore: Tinnitul is a Smoke Mephit in the employ of the Askanii-Reeds Trading Company. He is bound to the fireplace of the Akanii-Reeds Trading Company Building in Waterlane.

Tisereldanour.lore: Tisereldanour was a "great" Green Dragon and a draconic ruler. She ruled the area of the Illym Forest.

Tol.lore: Tol was the brother of god Urth. He was a mythical explorer of Askan who founded several towns along the River Torna, including Tolstad.

Toli Tolpare.lore: Toli Tolpare is a member of the Norvikian Resistance. He can usually be found in the Rollermill or Tavern with his friends.

Visken.lore: Visken is Tolstad's mortician and former dentist and surgeon. He is the intended first character that the player talks to, and provides useful information about the main quest, Cleric's immediate past and items, information about the city, and starts quests associated with him.

Warden Kullervo'Lämäläinen.lore: Kullervo is an Elf and a representative of Illym.

Ôst.lore: Ôst is a kobold in employment of the mortician Visken, for whom he acts as a guard. He can be found sitting at the exit from the upper lair of Visken's Lair.

Ûrth-Nisse.lore: Ûrth-Nisse is a member of the Norvikian Resistance. He can usually be found in the Rollermill or Tavern with his friends.

Bloodcity Bay.lore: Blodcity Bay is the largest city of Fell Tire. From the little information in the game, it seems to be home of Evil-aligned people such as Devils or Orcs.

Azgal Ashmask.lore: Azgal Ashmask was a low caste dwarf born in the tunnels of Lekm-Muraz. In 443 AE, he became the God of exiles and egalitarianism after starting a workers' revolution.

Illym.lore: Illym The Mender is an Elven goddess who established the Illym Empire. The Illym Forest is also named after her.

Lady Fair.lore: Lady Fair was a god, and an ascended halfling.

Shadow Lord.lore: A walking god who lived early in the Divine Era. He was most well known for his legion of Death Knights, and for their almost century long reign over Old Diminor.

Tokig.lore: Tokig the Gambler was a trickster god and an ascended goblin.

Urth.lore: Urth was the state god of Norvik, known by his epithet as the Father Of Norvik. He is The Cleric's god after whom the Urthguard are named, as well as the human ethnicity Urthfolk.

Yggeria.lore: Yggeria was the god of the Ym Elves.

Arcane Era.lore: The Fourth Era. It lasted for 511 years, ending 27 years prior to the events of the game.

Divine Era.lore: The Third Era. It lasted for 379 years.

Fade Era.lore: The Second Era. It was also known as the Fall Era.

Gate Era.lore: The first of the five eras, when Jor crafted Jor's Realm, and the gods fought to bring order. Succeeded by the Fade Era.

Postwar Era.lore: The current era, which has lasted twenty-seven years. It succeeds the Arcane Era.

Coast War.lore: A nearly century spanning conflict fought between the great nations of the Arcane Era. It was unimaginably destructive, with around a half of humankind perishing during it.

Arcanists.lore: Arcanists are a magocratic (i.e. autocracy of mages) political ideology, movement, and party that centers around anti-democratic rule of the most powerful wizards.

Azgalian Worker's Party.lore: Azgalian Worker's Party are a political party in Norvik, part of the Azgalist movement. They are labor-oriented and base their ideology in the ideas of its founder Azgal Ashmask.

Party Of Norvik.lore: Party Of Norvik (also known as The Nationalists) are a Norvikian political party and the current ruling party of the city.

Political Parties.lore: Esoteric Ebb features several political parties that struggle for influence in the newly democratic city of Norvik.

Revolutionary Freestriders.lore: Revolutionary Freestriders are a Norvikian political party and a wider ideology promoting electoral democracy and laissez-faire capitalism.

Arcanist.lore: Arcanist is one of six possible feats that can be obtained from the quest Election Week. It is related to the Arcanist political party, and is associated with Intelligence.

Azgalian Spirit.lore: Azgalian Spirit is one of two possible feats obtained from the quest Circle Square Conflict. It is ideologically related to the Azgalian Worker's Party.

Democratic Herald.lore: Democratic Herald is a feat obtained after completing the quest Democratization.

Future Patriarch.lore: Future Patriarch is one of two possible feats obtained from the quest Young & Dumb.

God-Emperor Wizard.lore: God-Emperor Wizard is one of six possible feats obtained from the quest Class Conflict. It comes from repeating that you are a Wizard, and is associated with Intelligence.

Lady Fair (Feat).lore: Lady Fair is one of two possible feats obtained from the quest Young & Dumb.

Money Talks.lore: Money Talks is one of two possible Feats obtained from the quest Circle Square Conflict.

Ironbrush.lore: Ironbrush is a character mentioned by Snell as the person who drew the green dragon graffiti on a wall of Visken's Lair, Tolstad South.

Askanii-Reeds Trading Company.lore: Askanii-Reeds Trading Company is a Freestrider-aligned business and organization, one of many throughout The Coast with multiple branches. In Tolstad their head-quarters are located in Waterlane.

Order of Arcane Clerics.lore: The Order of Arcane Clerics of Norvik (abbr. OACN, also known as The Towers) are a section of the Urthguard which specializes in spellcasting.

Urthguard.lore: Urthguard in Norvik are the main law-keeping organization; the guards of the city. They are linked to the religion of the god Urth.

Urth's Passing.lore: Urth's Passing is a religious observance related to the god Urth. It is a winter celebration where families feast together.

'Helm'.lore: The 'Helm' (also Great Helm in the file name) is the default Helmet. It is found on the player when they first wake up in Visken's basement.

Amethyst.lore: The Amethyst is a Trinket given by the Hail Snail after freeing him.

Aventail.lore: Aventail is a Helmet looted from the body of the Skeleton.

Basinet.lore: The Basinet is a Helmet found in the Secret Tunnel.

Book of the Mind, Sixth Edition.lore: The Book of the Mind is a piece of Literature. It may be acquired during the quest The Lost 6th Edition.

Bracers of Thievery.lore: The Bracers of Thievery are a Trinket found in Visken's Lair on a stone platform after the Burned Corpse is brought to it.

Broken Sword.lore: The Broken Sword is a Weapon found in the lower part of Visken's Lair, north-west of the room, on a barrel, after dealing with the Zombie (Visken's Lair).

Bronze Shield.lore: The Bronze Shield is a Trinket that can be found in front of an abandoned hut in the Undercoast Path.

Bucket.lore: The Bucket is a Helmet. It can be found in Tolstad South, on a platform above the Imposing Tree.

Caretaker.lore: The Caretaker is a Weapon held by The Duck. It can be pickpocketed at DC 22, however, doing this will trigger an interaction where Caretaker resists and The Duck takes it back.

Collected Chants of Norvik.lore: The Collected Chants of Norvik is a work of Literature located in on a bookshelf in Visken's Lair. It can read while on the bookshelf.

Concentrated Rhubarb Ale.lore: Concentrated Rhubarb Ale is a consumable that increases the Cleric's Charisma by +1 for a week. 2 bottle of Concentrated Rhubarb Ale can be found around Tolstad: one can be pickpocketed at DC 15 from Rixbiter, and one can be bought from the Potion Seller for 230 Crowns.

Crowns.lore: Urth Crowns. The official currency minted in Norvik's Riksbank.

Docile Stress Cube.lore: The Docile Stress Cube is a Trinket obtained after defeating the Gelatinous Cube.

Emerald.lore: The Emerald is a Trinket pickpocketed from Haelon Bondavol at DC 15. Alternatively, you can convince him to give it to you by mentioning Olzis wants it in order to make a gate key.

Esoteric Lockpick.lore: The Esoteric Lockpick is a Trinket located in the South Caverns. It is looted from the body of a headless rogue.

Esoteric Pocket Watch.lore: The Esoteric Pocket Watch is a Trinket found on Visken's desk in Visken's Lair. It is a pocket watch that is perpetually incorrect.

Flower Crown.lore: The Flower Crown is a Trinket obtained from Miss Nearly after completing the quest Essence of Inflorescence, or pickpocketed from her at DC 12.

Frog-Mouth.lore: The Frog-Mouth is a Helmet found atop a dresser in Darrow's Nest.

Garden Apple.lore: Garden Apple is a healing consumable. It is the basic healing item, healing only 1 HP.

Gauntlets of Ogre Power.lore: The Gauntlets of Ogre Power are a Trinket found in the Secret Tunnel.

Hex Weekly.lore: Hex Weekly is a piece of Literature than can be purchased from Arn for 20 Crowns. It can also be pickpocketed from him at DC 10.

Holy Symbol of Whitemane.lore: The Holy Symbol of Whitemane is a Trinket that is located in the Survey. It is hidden in the mouth of the large cat statue, and can be located by casting Detect Secrets.

Incense Bowl.lore: The Incense Bowl is a Trinket found in the City Below the City.

Ioun Stone, Insight.lore: The Ioun Stone, Insight is a Trinket given by a Brrr after saving him in the Undercoast Path.

Jacinth.lore: The Jacinth is a Trinket obtained after solving the 3 riddles of a Minotaur statue in the City Below the City.

Kattegatt.lore: Kattegatt is a Weapon found on the wall of the Askanii-Reeds Trading Company Building in Waterlane. It can be stolen with a DC 19 Dexterity check.

Light Crossbow.lore: The Light Crossbow is a Weapon belonging to Ôst. It can be obtained by convincing the Kobold to give it to you, or by pickpocketing him.

Mace of Terror.lore: The Mace of Terror is a Weapon found in the Crypt, behind the Skeleton.

Orange Ledger.lore: The Orange Ledger is a work of Literature located in

PPG Documents.lore: PPG Documents are a piece of Literature. They can be taken from Alfoz by succeeding on an Intelligence check, or pickpocketed from him at DC 9.

Petrified Longbow.lore: The Petrified Longbow is a Weapon found in the Roots. It is taken from the petrified body of a long-dead adventurer.

Pickaxe of Secrets.lore: The Pickaxe of Secrets is a Weapon obtained by talking to Akzel Mâddson after completing the quest Lost Mines of Toul'Amon. It is an ordinary pickaxe which has been enchanted with the spell Detect Secret.

Piece of Paper.lore: The Piece of Paper is a piece of Literature found on a corpse in the City Below the City.

Pixie Dust Slush.lore: Pixie Dust Slush is a consumable that increases the Cleric's Dexterity by +1 for a week. 4 Pixie Dust Slushes can be found around Tolstad: one can be stolen from a cupboard within the Askanii-Reeds Trading Company Building, one can be pickpocketed at DC 14 from Miska Sageleaf, one can be found in...

Potion of Healing.lore: Potions of Healing are healing Consumables found in various places around Tolstad and the City Below. One Potion of Healing can be found in a crate of potions in The Sewers.

Resistance Note.lore: The Resistance Note is a piece of Literature. It can be found on the roof of the Rollermill.

Ring of Protection.lore: The Ring of Protection is a Trinket that can be found in a birds nest in a tree in Southern Tolstad.

Sallet.lore: The Sallet is a Helmet found in the Askanii Reeds Trading Company Warehouse, in a crate of Mosquito Sand.

Sending Pebble.lore: The Sending Pebble is a Trinket found in the dark room on the ground floor of the Rollermill. It can be used to send short messages to various other characters.

Simple Hammer.lore: The Simple Hammer is a Weapon. It can be found on the floor outside of Visken's Lair after resolving Circle Square Conflict.

Sloshy Stress Cube.lore: The Sloshy Stress Cube is a Trinket.

Snell's Notepad.lore: Snell's Notepad is an equipable Key item held by Snell. It can be pickpocketed at DC 7.

Spectral Halberd.lore: The Spectral Halberd is a Weapon obtained after inserting the Jacinth into the Whitemane Shrine in the South Caverns.

Strange Mushroom Juice.lore: Strange Mushroom Juice is a consumable that increases the Cleric's Wisdom by +1 for a week. Two bottles of Strange Mushroom Juice can be found across Tolstad: one can be taken from a nature spirit beneath the Imposing Tree, provided the player has Druidcraft, and one can be bought from the Potion...

Stress Cube.lore: The Stress Cube is a Trinket obtained after passing a dexterity check in upper level of the Northwest Tolstad Rollermill.

Tavern Key.lore: They Tavern Key is an equipable Key Item given by Olzis in the Drunk Sphinx during dialogue.

The Azgal Chant.lore: The Azgal Chant is a piece of Literature. It is a local Azgalian newspaper.

The Mind Expansion.lore: The Mind Expansion is a consumable that increases the Cleric's Intelligence by +1 for a week. Two bottles of The Mind Expansion can be found across Tolstad: one can be pickpocketed at DC 15 from Visken, and one can be bought from the Potion Seller for 290 Crowns.

The Undercommon.lore: The Undercommon is an equippable piece of Literature. It can be found in the Tea Shop on a burnt bookshelf.

Tiny Dictator.lore: The Tiny Dictator is a Trinket found in Snurre's Office.

Tol's Sword.lore: The Tol's Sword is a Weapon found after using the Deck of Many Things, and receiving permanent disadvantage on everything.

Tower Key.lore: The Tower Key is an equipable Key Item given by Nicholas Darrow during dialogue. It can also be taken from him without talking to him.

Troll Tooth.lore: The Troll Tooth is a Trinket left behind by Big Leader Gourgalash after he is defeated.

Unluck Blade.lore: The Unluck Blade is a Weapon located in the Undercoast Path. It is dropped by the Assassin after they are defeated for the final time.

Voting Card.lore: The Voting Card is a Literature item. It can be found in the lower layer of Visken's Lair, next to the staircase leading up.

Vourgeni River Droplets.lore: The Vourgeni River Droplets are a consumable that increases the Cleric's Strength by +1 for a week. 3 bottles of Vourgeni River Droplets can be found across Tolstad: one under a stone in Waterlane that can be detected by Find Secrets, one that can be bought from the Potion Seller for 220 Crowns, ...

Vyz-Kha Ginger Drink.lore: The Vyz-Kha Ginger Drink is a consumable that increases the Cleric's Constitution by +1 for a week. 2 bottles of Vyz-Kha Ginger Drink can be found across Tolstad: one in a crate behind the Griffon near the Askanii-Reeds Trading Company Building, and one that can be bought from the Potion Seller f...

Wisdom Tooth.lore: The Wisdom Tooth is a Trinket held by the Zombie that can be pickpocketed at DC 15, or looted after knocking the zombie out.

Wood Shield.lore: The Wood Shield is a Trinket that belongs to Moongore. It can be obtained by pickpocketing her.

Lady Chamar.lore: Lady Chamăr is a human who came to Tolstad from the Holy Mountain. She doesn't play any major role in the demo, and her purpose is enigmatic.

Askanii.lore: Askanii (sometimes Askan) is one of the eight bands along The Coast. It neighbors Reeds and Divinii Torum.

Askanii-Reeds Trading Company Building.lore: The Askanii-Reeds Trading Company Building is a location in Waterlane. It is owned by the Askanii-Reeds Trading Company.

Bands.lore: Eight esoterically divided geographical areas that makes up The Coast. Each band is distinct in both geography and inhabitants.

Doom.lore: The Doom is a sub-area of the City Below the City. It can be accessed through a hidden door in the Crypt.

Esoteric Pockets.lore: Pockets of space where various different Esoteric effects apply. They are rarely found around the Coast, but become increasingly prevalent the closer one gets to the Infinite Wastes.

Fordnip Commonwealth.lore: The Fordnip Commonwealth is one of the nations on the Askan, home of the halflings. Category:Locations

Forge of the Furies.lore: The Forge of the Furies is an Esoteric Pocket containing a sliver of the elemental plane of fire.

Hae'Xi.lore: Hae'Xi is one of the eight bands that makes up The Coast. Its name means "Growing Maw" or "Into the Air" in abyssal.

Lake Cujah.lore: A salt water lake located in Hae'Xi. It is connected with a similar body of water in the Undercoast, and is the only body of water aside from Jor's Sea to pass the Gravity Flip.

Reeds.lore: Reeds is one of the eight bands along The Coast. It is viewed as the homeland of Humans.

River Torna.lore: A river in Askanii. It is the second longest known river on the Coast, and splits Askanii in half.

Secret Tunnel.lore: The Secret Tunnel is a location in Tolstad North. It is a small tunnel that connects the Temple of Urth to the Tea Shop.

Snell's Pad.lore: Snell's Pad is a location in the Holes, a series of stacked underground apartments near Waterlane. It is where Snell lives.

Snurre's Office.lore: Snurre's Office is a location in Waterlane. It is an office owned by Snurre Appledent, who can be found within it.

South Caverns.lore: The South Caverns are a sub-section of the City Below the City. They are inhabited by a Roper, and are located in the domain of the HWBT.

Temple of Urth.lore: Temple of Urth is a location in Tolstad North. It is a small religious site dedicated to the god Urth, now housing the upcoming elections and an entrance to The City Below.

Thazen.lore: Thazen was the greatest of the floating cities. During the Coast War, it was used as a center of operations by the Haagin-Dim Coalition.

The Coast.lore: The Esoteric Coast (also known as Realm of Jor, Jor's Realm, or JOR) is the name of the dimension created by the wizard Jor. It is the world in which Esoteric Ebb takes place.

The Sewers.lore: The Sewers are a sublocation of the City Below the City.

Tolstad.lore: Tolstad is the central district of Norvik, and the setting of the game. It is an island fortress on River Torna.

Vourgeni.lore: Vourgeni is one of the eight bands along The Coast. It neighbors Hae'Xi and Reeds.

Vyz-Kha.lore: The Vyz-Kha Kingdoms, or VK for short, are a collection of loose dwarven cities in northwestern Askanii.

Ym.lore: Ym is one of the eight Bands that makes up The Coast. It is covered in artificial forests, and crags covered in eternal darkness.

Cold Corpse.lore: Cold Corpse is an interactable corpse in the lower part of Visken's Lair. It starts the quest Three Hundred Pieces.

Croland.lore: Croland is mentioned by Visken as a bookbinder residing in Waterlane View of Tolstad, related to the quest Phoenix Folio. A poster can be find in South Tolstad:

Esoteric Ebb.lore: Esoteric Ebb is a narrative cRPG by Christoffer Bodegård (studio Sudden Snail) and collaborators including studios Gibbet Games and Speldosa Interactive, published by Raw Fury. Its game system is based on Dungeons & Dragons and heavily inspired by Disco Elysium and Planescape TormentC.

Esoteric Ebb (Phenomenon).lore: The name of a phenomenon causing high-level magic to slowly be drained from Jor's Realm. It can be learned about by speaking to Haeron Bondalov and passing a passive Intelligence check.

Goblon.lore: Goblon is an elusive graffiti artist famous in Norvik.

Helmet.lore: Helmets are a type of equipable Item in Esoteric Ebb.

Jor.lore: Jor was a powerful wizard who created the dimension of The Coast, which is why it's also called Jor's Realm or simply JOR.

Literature.lore: Literature is a type of Equipable Item in Estoric Ebb.

Necklace of Fireballs.lore: A magic item that was mass-produced during the Coast War. They were necklaces with a number of beads on them.

Norvikian Resistance.lore: Norvikian Resistance is a major quest in Esoteric Ebb.

Quest.lore: Quests offer XP, rewards, and opportunities to explore and affect the world. Esoteric Ebb's main quest is The Tea Shop that Blew Up, but there are also dozens of side quests (some tracked, some not).

Thalassos.lore: Thalassos is a merman entrepreneur based in Tolstad. He is not present in the demo, only mentioned by Arn as the owner of his kiosk.

Toulin'catl.lore: The mollusca Toulin'catl were an ancient Askanii civilization of snailfolk. They disappeared during the Fade Era.

Undercoast.lore: Undersea Coast is a part of the Jor's Realm that is found underneath its surface.

Diminorii.lore: The Diminorii are one of the most powerful magocratic families in Old Diminor. They were the descendants of Doug Doulin Dim, the Drake Slayer King.

House Hagginshags.lore: House Hagginshags was a powerful magocratic family in Old Diminor. They were the descendants of the Shadow Lord.

House Prax.lore: House Prax is a magocractic family. The last living member of the family is Rubrick Prax, the head of The Arcane Circle, an Arcanist organization.

Abilities.lore: Abilities are Esoteric Ebb's primary character statistics — six attributes which represent how good a character is at given tasks, namely by modifying values of a dice roll. This system is based on Dungeons & Dragons 5th Edition, although streamlined.

Cantrip.lore: Cantrip in Esoteric Ebb and Dungeons & Dragons 5th Edition is a "0th level" spell available at first level. It does not consume a spell slot, so it can be cast freely, unlimited by spellcasting rules of resting.

Encounters.lore: Encounters are turn-based conflicts (not necessarily physical combat as in Dungeons & Dragons).

Hit points.lore: Hit Points (abbreviated to HP) is a representation of a character's health; i.e. points that decrease if taking damage (often also from social effects such as embarrassment), and which will trigger a Death Saving Throw when they run out.

Journal.lore: The Journal is one of the tabs found to the left of the screen where, when clicked, the map of the present area is displayed and dicovered Glossary Entries are found. Category: Mechanics

Roper.lore: The Roper is a hostile monster located in the South Caverns. It is initially disguised as a stalagmite, but will awaken and attack the Cleric if they wander close to it.

Zombie (Visken's Lair).lore: In the lower half of Visken's Lair on day 1, a zombie is interacting with the pile of apples.

Deck of Many Things.lore: The Deck of Many Things is a canonically Legendary magic item in both Esoteric Ebb and Dungeons & Dragons 5th edition media. You can acquire the Deck of Many Things by solving the Gynosphinx's second riddle as part of the quest An Easy Riddle.

Charisma.lore: Charisma (abbr. CHA) is a character statistic, and a character in The Cleric's mind.

Constitution.lore: Constitution (abbr. CON) is a character statistic, and a character in The Cleric's mind.

Dexterity.lore: Dexterity (abbr. DEX) is a character statistic, and a character in The Cleric's mind.

Great Perch.lore: The Great Perch is a drake-like animal that, due to its association with Urth, is used as a symbol of Norvik. Category:Animals

Intelligence.lore: Intelligence (abbr. INT) is a character statistic, and a character in The Cleric's mind.

Strength.lore: Strength (abbr. STR) is a character statistic, and a character in The Cleric's mind.

The Conquest.lore: The term for the human founding of the city-state of Norvik, in 468 AE. Category:History

Wisdom.lore: Wisdom (abbr. WIS) is a character statistic, and a character in The Cleric's mind.

An Easy Riddle.lore: An Easy Riddle is a major quest in Esoteric Ebb.

Coinlord's Meet.lore: Coinlord's Meet is a minor quest in Esoteric Ebb.
related: The Tea Shop that Blew Up

Copper Press Fever.lore: Copper Press Fever is a major quest in Esoteric Ebb.

Cult Hit.lore: Cult Hit is minor quest in Esoteric Ebb.

Dragon of Tolstad.lore: Dragon of Tolstad is a minor quest in Esoteric Ebb.
related: The Tea Shop that Blew Up

Elven Connection.lore: Elven Connection is a major quest in Esoteric Ebb.

Golden Line.lore: Golden Line is a minor quest in Esoteric Ebb.

Hammer and Zeal.lore: Hammer and Zeal is minor quest in Esoteric Ebb.

Lichhouse Blues.lore: Lichhouse Blues is  major quest in Esoteric Ebb.

Missing Paladin.lore: Missing Paladin is a minor quest in Esoteric Ebb.

Phoenix Folio.lore: Phoenix Folio is a minor quest in Esoteric Ebb.

River Take Ye.lore: River Take Ye is minor quest in Esoteric Ebb.

Safe & Secure.lore: Safe & Secure is a minor quest in Esoteric Ebb.

The Lost 6th Edition.lore: The Lost 6th Edition is a minor quest in Esoteric Ebb.

The Tea Shop that Blew Up.lore: The Tea Shop that Blew Up is the first and main quest of the game.

Three Hundred Pieces.lore: Three Hundred Pieces Ye is minor quest in Esoteric Ebb.

Valkyrie.lore: Valkyrie is a minor quest in Esoteric Ebb.

Young and Dumb.lore: Young and Dumb is a major quest in Esoteric Ebb.

Elves.lore: Elves is one of the races populating Jor's Realm. There are several sorts of elves, like Illym Elves or Pimeä Elves, but they are all characterised by their pointed ears.

Goblins.lore: Goblins are a short humanoid species found in large number in Tolstad's southern quarter and underground.

Whitemane.lore: A tribe of feline-folk who ruled portions of Askanii during the Divine Era.

Aura of Vitality.lore: Aura of Vitality is a 3rd-level, Primal spell.

Caelum's Mage Hand.lore: Caelum's Mage Hand is a cantrip that can be obtained from using Comprehend Languages on a spell tome within the upper level of the Visken's Lair. It is a modified version of Mage Hand that has been created by Caelum Diminorii.

Charm Person.lore: Charm Person is a utility spell. It is given by Visken during dialogue with him, or can be pickpocketed from him.

Comprehend Languages.lore: Comprehend Languages is a utility spell. It can be purchased from the Storm Gnome vendor for 100 Crowns.

Crusader's Mantle.lore: Crusader's Mantle is an Evocation spell. It can be found in the City Below, buried under some rocks that can be noticed by Detect Secret.

Cure Wounds.lore: Cure Wounds is a basic healing spell. It is the earliest and easiest spell to acquire, found sticking out of the pile of apples in Visken's Lair where The Cleric first wakes up.

Dancing Light.lore: Dancing Light is a cantrip that can be obtained from

Detect Secrets.lore: Detect Secrets is a utility spell. It can be extracted from the Pickaxe of Secrets with a DC 14 Intelligence check.

Divine Hex.lore: Divine Hex is a Spell. It is obtained by forming a Warlock Pact with Ever-Glad.

Druidcraft.lore: Druidcraft is a cantrip that can be obtained from Nessan after passing a DC 20 Wisdom check. The DC is lowered by 5 when Nessan "Finds you reasonable" (based on the answer to her question of sustainability) and by 5 when "She pities you and your hungry stomach" (based on an answer where The Cleri...

Inflict Wounds.lore: Inflict Wounds is a basic damaging spell. It is given by The Duck during dialogue, or can be pickpocketed from him at DC 10.

Revivify.lore: Revivify is a necromantic spell. It can be found in Visken's Lair, behind an illusory wall that can be detected by Detect Secrets.

Sending.lore: Sending is a third level Evocation spell. A weakened version of it can be cast using the Trinket Sending Pebble.

Speak With Dead.lore: Speak With Dead is a necromantic spell. It can be taught to you by Haelon Bondavol.

Thaumaturgy.lore: Thaumaturgy is a cantrip. It can be taken off of The Duck in the Crypt at Night after defeating the Skeleton.

Antesepalian.lore: "Antesepalian" means "before the closing of the gates", used to describe civilization during the Gate Era, before Jor closed the portals to The Coast, 2027 years before the events of the game.
